lyrics:
In love
I couldn't see the walls
Only the candle
Only the fall
Of love
I saved every sign
The curve of a shoulder
The hour, the wine

In is a room
Of is a ghost
With is the road
I needed most
Time has a window
Place has a door
Direction is asking
What love is for

Say it slowly
Say it true
What am I
Beside you
Not your shadow
Not your claim
Just a hand
Beside your name

In love
We vanished from view
The city kept moving
But I only knew you
Of love
We made our museum
Tickets and photographs
Proof we had been

In is a room
Of is a ghost
With is the road
I needed most
Time has a window
Place has a door
Direction is asking
What love is for

Every sentence
Needs somewhere to land
Every silence
Needs a hand
Not behind you
Not ahead
Let the living
Wake the dead

With love
There's room to remain
No golden forever
No beautiful chain
With love
We move and we bend
Not toward possession
But toward what can mend

In is a room
Of is a ghost
With is the road
I needed most
Time has a window
Place has a door
Direction is asking
What love is for

So let the old words
Fall where they fall
Love is not ownership
Love is not all
Love is the distance
We still travel through
In me, of me
With me and you

In love
Of love
With love
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track creates a warm, intimate, and reflective atmosphere with a light, open texture featuring an acoustic jazz combo of piano, upright bass, brushed drums, and a clean electric guitar. The energy is low and laid-back, driven by a gentle, conversational swing feel and a soft, breathy female vocal that feels very personal. The production is excellent, creating a spacious and natural-sounding mix where each instrument has clarity and sits comfortably in its own place. The track is defined by its tasteful musicianship and interplay, highlighted by a lyrical and slightly bluesy guitar solo that reinforces the song's gentle, thoughtful mood.

review.review_text: PCBender turns grammar into grief and grace here, building an entire meditation on love out of prepositions: 'In is a room / Of is a ghost / With is the road I needed most.' That conceit could tip into cleverness for its own sake, but the waltz-time groove keeps it human, the brushed swing feel loose enough that the lyric lands like a conversation rather than a lecture. The final turn, 'Love is not ownership / Love is not all / Love is the distance we still travel through,' earns its weight the way a good John Hiatt line does, plainspoken and quietly devastating. A highlight.
